text:
Looks promising... but it creates the inverse problem: I need to type my instance's URL, unless it's in the predefined list, which is already a bit too long for my taste.
I don't know if this can be avoided. I am always logged in into my instance, so maybe this leaves an exploitable trace in my browser? Though I'd almost prefer that it doesn't (for privacy reasons).

text: Hi, I've finally found time to make a small video.. I am showing a flow that represents a very small crud-application where I reconnect multiple connections at once as well as connections being animated when they are retriggering (like when using a timer). Hope you like it! <https://youtu.be/N8gIblu1dgs|https://youtu.be/N8gIblu1dgs>

text: interesting take on using the nodes as kind of a user interface. Do you see this as something that end users could use or more of a debugging feature?

elements.text: interesting take on using the nodes as kind of a user interface. Do you see this as something that end users could use or more of a debugging feature?

text: Hi, no these flows are not for end-users. Although I think these can be used in the broad development process and not just by "flow developers", also for analysts and other roles.. offcourse the flows that are shown are very simple, I've already got examples based on real scenario's which are much more complex. In the near future I'll demonstrate how these flows can be translated to a UI for end-users
